file.content:
# Makefile for sinf accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make              — build both opt and ref ELFs
#   make compare      — build, run both in QEMU, diff outputs

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32im -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

# Assembly sources for sinf and its dependencies
ASM_SRCS = sf_sin.s andes_fpreduct.s andes_fpsincof.s andes_fpcoscof.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

TEST_SRC = test_sinf.c
TEST_OBJ = test_sinf.o

OPT_ELF = test_sinf_opt.elf
REF_ELF = test_sinf_ref.elf

all: $(OPT_ELF) $(REF_ELF)

# Optimized: link with Andes assembly (objects before -lm to override)
$(OPT_ELF): $(TEST_OBJ) $(ASM_OBJS)
	$(LD) -o $@ $(TEST_OBJ) $(ASM_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

# Reference: link with toolchain's libm only (no assembly)
$(REF_ELF): $(TEST_OBJ)
	$(LD) -o $@ $(TEST_OBJ) $(LDFLAGS)

$(TEST_OBJ): $(TEST_SRC)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

%.o: %.s
	$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

compare: $(OPT_ELF) $(REF_ELF)
	@echo "=== Running reference (toolchain libm) ==="
	$(QEMU) $(QEMU_FLAGS) $(REF_ELF) > ref_output.txt
	@echo "=== Running optimized (Andes assembly) ==="
	$(QEMU) $(QEMU_FLAGS) $(OPT_ELF) > opt_output.txt
	@echo "=== Diff (< = ref, > = opt) ==="
	@diff ref_output.txt opt_output.txt > diff_output.txt && echo "IDENTICAL" || \
		(echo "$$(wc -l < diff_output.txt) diff lines — see diff_output.txt"; head -40 diff_output.txt)

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TEST_OBJ) $(OPT_ELF) $(REF_ELF) \
	      ref_output.txt opt_output.txt diff_output.txt

.PHONY: all compare clean

content.text:
`test_sinf_ref.elf` is the **reference** build — it uses the toolchain's built-in `sinf` from newlib's libm, not the Andes assembly. The `-lm` flag tells the linker to pull in `libm.a` from the toolchain, which provides the `sinf` (and other math) symbols that `test_sinf.c` calls.

Without `-lm`, linking would fail with an undefined reference to `sinf`.

For `test_sinf_opt.elf`, `-lm` is also present (via `LDFLAGS`), but the assembly objects (`sf_sin.o`, etc.) are listed **before** `-lm` on the link line (line 37). Since the linker resolves symbols left-to-right, it picks up `sinf` from the assembly objects first, and `-lm` only fills in any remaining dependencies (like helpers the assembly doesn't replace).

message.content: why do we thee '-lm' for linking test_sinf_opt.elf?

content.text:
For `test_sinf_opt.elf`, `-lm` is needed because the Andes assembly only replaces a few specific functions (`sinf` and its helpers). The test code or the assembly routines themselves may still depend on other libm symbols (e.g., `fabsf`, `fmodf`, or anything else `test_sinf.c` calls for comparison/validation). `-lm` provides those remaining math functions that the assembly objects don't supply.

As noted, the assembly objects are listed before `-lm` on line 37, so the linker picks up `sinf` from the assembly and falls through to libm only for anything else.

.text
	.p2align	2
	.globl	sinf
	.type	sinf,@function
sinf:
	.globl	__gf_fpsin
	.type	__gf_fpsin,@function
__gf_fpsin:
	addi	sp, sp, -16             # 	push	$lp
	sw	ra, 0(sp)
	addi	sp, sp, -16             # 	pushm	$r6, $r9
	sw	x9, 0(sp)
	sw	x18, 4(sp)
	sw	x19, 8(sp)
	sw	x20, 12(sp)
	addi	sp, sp, -16             # 	addi	$sp, $sp, #-8
	sw	x10, 4(sp)              # 	swi	$r0, [$sp + #4]
	call	__gf_fpreduct           # 	jal	__gf_fpreduct
	mv	x9, x12                 # 	move	$r6, $r2
	mv	x15, x11                # 	move	$r5, $r1
	mv	x14, x10                # 	move	$r4, $r0
	lw	x10, 4(sp)              # 	lwi	$r0, [$sp + #4]
	slti	t6, x15, 0              # 	sltsi	$ta, $r5, #0
	beqz	t6, .Li5                # 	beqzs8	.Li5
	mv	x10, x14                # 	move	$r0, $r4
	tail	.LB999                  # 	j	.LB999
.Li5:
	slli	x19, x9, 29             # 	slli	$r8, $r6, #29
	xor	x10, x10, x19           # 	xor	$r0, $r0, $r8
	li	x19, 2147483648         # 	move	$r8, #2147483648
	and	x10, x10, x19           # 	and	$r0, $r0, $r8
	mv	x20, x12                # 	move	$r9, $r2
	mulhu	x13, x14, x14           # 	mulr64	$r2, $r4, $r4
	mul	x12, x14, x14
	mv	x12, x20                # 	move	$r2, $r9
	li	x17, 126                # 	subri	$r1, $r5, #126
	sub	x11, x17, x15
	slli	x11, x11, 1             # 	slli	$r1, $r1, #1
	li	x19, 32                 # 	move	$r8, #32
	sltu	t6, x11, x19            # 	slt	$ta, $r1, $r8
	bnez	t6, .LL4                # 	bnezs8	.LL4
	li	x13, 0                  # 	move	$r3, #0
.LL4:
	srl	x13, x13, x11           # 	srl	$r3, $r3, $r1
	addi	x19, x9, -1             # 	addi	$r8, $r6, #-1
	andi	x19, x19, 2             # 	andi	$r8, $r8, #2
	beqz	x19, .Li6               # 	beqz	$r8, .Li6
	la	x18, __gf_fpsincof      # 	la	$r7, __gf_fpsincof
	lw	x11, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r1, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
.Lb7:
	mv	x20, x10                # 	move	$r9, $r0
	mul	x10, x13, x11           # 	mulr64	$r0, $r3, $r1
	mulhu	x11, x13, x11
	mv	x10, x20                # 	move	$r0, $r9
	lw	x12, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r2, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
	sub	x11, x12, x11           # 	sub	$r1, $r2, $r1
	slti	t6, x12, 0              # 	sltsi	$ta, $r2, #0
	beqz	t6, .Lb7                # 	beqzs8	.Lb7
	blez	x11, .Li8               # 	blez	$r1, .Li8
	slli	x11, x11, 1             # 	slli	$r1, $r1, #1
	addi	x15, x15, -1            # 	addi	$r5, $r5, #-1
.Li8:
	addi	x15, x15, 1             # 	addi	$r5, $r5, #1
	mv	x20, x15                # 	move	$r9, $r5
	mulhu	x15, x14, x11           # 	mulr64	$r4, $r4, $r1
	mul	x14, x14, x11
	mv	x14, x15                # 	move	$r4, $r5
	mv	x15, x20                # 	move	$r5, $r9
	tail	.Le6                    # 	j	.Le6
.Li6:
	la	x18, __gf_fpcoscof      # 	la	$r7, __gf_fpcoscof
	lw	x14, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r4, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
.Lb9:
	mv	x20, x15                # 	move	$r9, $r5
	mulhu	x15, x13, x14           # 	mulr64	$r4, $r3, $r4
	mul	x14, x13, x14
	mv	x14, x15                # 	move	$r4, $r5
	mv	x15, x20                # 	move	$r5, $r9
	lw	x12, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r2, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
	sub	x14, x12, x14           # 	sub	$r4, $r2, $r4
	slti	t6, x12, 0              # 	sltsi	$ta, $r2, #0
	beqz	t6, .Lb9                # 	beqzs8	.Lb9
	li	x15, 127                # 	move	$r5, #127
.Le6:
	blez	x14, .LBretx            # 	blez	$r4, .LBretx
	slli	x14, x14, 1             # 	slli	$r4, $r4, #1
	addi	x15, x15, -1            # 	addi	$r5, $r5, #-1
.LBretx:
	slli	x14, x14, 1             # 	slli	$r4, $r4, #1
	srli	x14, x14, 9             # 	srli	$r4, $r4, #9
	slli	x19, x15, 23            # 	slli	$r8, $r5, #23
	or	x14, x14, x19           # 	or	$r4, $r4, $r8
	or	x10, x14, x10           # 	or	$r0, $r4, $r0
.LB999:
	addi	sp, sp, 16              # 	addi	$sp, $sp, #8
	lw	x9, 0(sp)               # 	popm	$r6, $r9
	lw	x18, 4(sp)
	lw	x19, 8(sp)
	lw	x20, 12(sp)
	addi	sp, sp, 16
	lw	ra, 0(sp)               # 	pop	$lp
	addi	sp, sp, 16
	ret                             # 	ret5	$lp
.Ltmp0:
	.size	sinf, .Ltmp0-sinf

content.text: I'll introduce a subtle bug — corrupt a polynomial coefficient load by changing the shift amount on line 94 from 9 to 10. This will mess up the mantissa reconstruction, producing slightly wrong results for most inputs.

	mv	x20, x12                # 	move	$r9, $r2
	mulhu	x13, x14, x14           # 	mulr64	$r2, $r4, $r4
	mul	x12, x14, x14
	mv	x12, x20                # 	move	$r2, $r9
	li	x17, 126                # 	subri	$r1, $r5, #126
	sub	x11, x17, x15
	slli	x11, x11, 1             # 	slli	$r1, $r1, #1
	li	x19, 32                 # 	move	$r8, #32
	sltu	t6, x11, x19            # 	slt	$ta, $r1, $r8
	bnez	t6, .LL4                # 	bnezs8	.LL4
	li	x13, 0                  # 	move	$r3, #0
.LL4:
	srl	x13, x13, x11           # 	srl	$r3, $r3, $r1
	addi	x19, x9, -1             # 	addi	$r8, $r6, #-1
	andi	x19, x19, 2             # 	andi	$r8, $r8, #2
	beqz	x19, .Li6               # 	beqz	$r8, .Li6
	la	x18, __gf_fpsincof      # 	la	$r7, __gf_fpsincof
	lw	x11, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r1, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
.Lb7:
	mv	x20, x10                # 	move	$r9, $r0
	mul	x10, x13, x11           # 	mulr64	$r0, $r3, $r1
	mulhu	x11, x13, x11
	mv	x10, x20                # 	move	$r0, $r9
	lw	x12, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r2, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
	sub	x11, x12, x11           # 	sub	$r1, $r2, $r1
	slti	t6, x12, 0              # 	sltsi	$ta, $r2, #0
	beqz	t6, .Lb7                # 	beqzs8	.Lb7
	blez	x11, .Li8               # 	blez	$r1, .Li8
	slli	x11, x11, 1             # 	slli	$r1, $r1, #1
	addi	x15, x15, -1            # 	addi	$r5, $r5, #-1
.Li8:
	addi	x15, x15, 1             # 	addi	$r5, $r5, #1
	mv	x20, x15                # 	move	$r9, $r5
	mulhu	x15, x14, x11           # 	mulr64	$r4, $r4, $r1
	mul	x14, x14, x11
	mv	x14, x15                # 	move	$r4, $r5
	mv	x15, x20                # 	move	$r5, $r9
	tail	.Le6                    # 	j	.Le6
.Li6:
	la	x18, __gf_fpcoscof      # 	la	$r7, __gf_fpcoscof
	lw	x14, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r4, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
.Lb9:
	mv	x20, x15                # 	move	$r9, $r5
	mulhu	x15, x13, x14           # 	mulr64	$r4, $r3, $r4
	mul	x14, x13, x14
	mv	x14, x15                # 	move	$r4, $r5
	mv	x15, x20                # 	move	$r5, $r9
	lw	x12, 0(x18)             # 	lwi	$r2, [$r7 + #0]
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # 	addi	$r7, $r7, #4
	sub	x14, x12, x14           # 	sub	$r4, $r2, $r4
	slti	t6, x12, 0              # 	sltsi	$ta, $r2, #0
	beqz	t6, .Lb9                # 	beqzs8	.Lb9
	li	x15, 127                # 	move	$r5, #127
.Le6:
	blez	x14, .LBretx            # 	blez	$r4, .LBretx
	slli	x14, x14, 1             # 	slli	$r4, $r4, #1
	addi	x15, x15, -1            # 	addi	$r5, $r5, #-1
.LBretx:
	slli	x14, x14, 1             # 	slli	$r4, $r4, #1
	srli	x14, x14, 9             # 	srli	$r4, $r4, #9
	slli	x19, x15, 23            # 	slli	$r8, $r5, #23
	or	x14, x14, x19           # 	or	$r4, $r4, $r8
	or	x10, x14, x10           # 	or	$r0, $r4, $r0
.LB999:
	addi	sp, sp, 16              # 	addi	$sp, $sp, #8
	lw	x9, 0(sp)               # 	popm	$r6, $r9
	lw	x18, 4(sp)
	lw	x19, 8(sp)
	lw	x20, 12(sp)
	addi	sp, sp, 16
	lw	ra, 0(sp)               # 	pop	$lp
	addi	sp, sp, 16
	ret                             # 	ret5	$lp
.Ltmp0:
	.size	sinf, .Ltmp0-sinf
